method_atom: A nonlinear stabilization scheme for discrete numerical solvers that suppresses spurious high-frequency oscillations near steep gradients without smearing the underlying sharp features, by monitoring a single scalar functional measuring a state's deviation from local equilibrium and selectively acting on it in one of two ways: (i) restriction, capping the deviation to a threshold, or (ii) filtering, smoothing the deviation field. The mechanism yields controllable, quantitatively estimable added dissipation and preserves smooth regions, playing the same regularizing role that limiter techniques play for other discretization schemes.
object_atom: Lattice Boltzmann simulations of fluid flow, tested on athermal 1D shock tubes with a 1:2 density ratio and a 2D lid-driven cavity at Reynolds numbers 2000-7500 on a coarse 100x100 grid, using both entropic and non-entropic quasiequilibria.
brief_reason: The threshold-triggered scalar limiting/filtering machinery for damping numerical oscillations with bounded dissipation carries across to any discretization method, while the nonequilibrium-entropy quantity and the lattice Boltzmann fluid-flow benchmarks are the domain-specific object.
